sections.title: Present Participle (वर्तमान कृदंत) — ता हुआ / ती हुई
sections.explanation:
The present participle describes an ongoing action and functions as an adjective modifying a noun, or as part of a progressive construction. It is formed by combining the imperfective participle (ता/ती/ते) with हुआ/हुई/हुए.

FORMATION:
Verb stem + ता/ती/ते + हुआ/हुई/हुए

The form agrees with the noun it modifies in gender and number:
  MASCULINE SINGULAR: दौड़ता हुआ लड़का = the running boy
  FEMININE SINGULAR: दौड़ती हुई लड़की = the running girl
  MASCULINE PLURAL: दौड़ते हुए लड़के = the running boys
  FEMININE PLURAL: दौड़ती हुई लड़कियाँ = the running girls

USAGE:
1. AS AN ADJECTIVE (modifying a noun):
  गाता हुआ आदमी = the singing man
  सोती हुई बच्ची = the sleeping girl

2. EXPRESSING SIMULTANEOUS ACTION ('while doing'):
  वह हँसते हुए बोला। = He spoke while laughing.
  वह रोती हुई आई। = She came crying.

Note: हुआ/हुई/हुए is sometimes dropped in casual speech:
  दौड़ता लड़का = the running boy (shorter form)
sections.tip: The present participle always agrees with the noun it modifies, NOT with the subject of the sentence. Think of it as an adjective derived from a verb — and Hindi adjectives agree with their nouns.

examples.hi: गाती हुई लड़की बहुत सुंदर है।
examples.en: The singing girl is very beautiful.

examples.hi: खेलते हुए बच्चे शोर मचा रहे थे।
examples.en: The playing children were making noise.

exercises.prompt: "दौड़ती हुई लड़की" — कृदंत स्त्रीलिंग क्यों है? (Why is the participle feminine?)
options: Because दौड़ना is a feminine verb
Because it agrees with लड़की, which is feminine
Because all participles are feminine by default
exercises.explanation: The present participle acts as an adjective and agrees with the noun it modifies. लड़की is feminine singular, so the participle takes the feminine form: दौड़ती हुई.

exercises.prompt: गाता _____ आदमी कौन है? (Who is the singing man?)
exercises.hint: Masculine singular form of होना participle
exercises.explanation: आदमी is masculine singular, so the present participle takes हुआ: गाता हुआ आदमी.

exercises.prompt: "वह रोती हुई आई" — what does this sentence mean?
options: She came while crying. / She came crying.
exercises.explanation: The present participle रोती हुई here expresses a simultaneous action: she came while crying / she came in a state of crying.

sections.title: Past Participle (भूत कृदंत) — हुआ / हुई
sections.explanation:
The past participle describes a completed action and is used as an adjective modifying a noun. It is formed from the perfective participle of the verb.

FORMATION:
Verb stem + आ/ई/ए (perfective form), often combined with हुआ/हुई/हुए for emphasis.

COMMON FORMS:
  टूटा हुआ = broken (टूटना, to break)
  खुला हुआ = open (खुलना, to open)
  बंद = closed (बंद करना/होना)
  लिखा हुआ = written (लिखना, to write)
  बना हुआ = made/built (बनना, to be made)
  गिरा हुआ = fallen (गिरना, to fall)
  सड़ा हुआ = rotten (सड़ना, to rot)

AGREEMENT:
Like all Hindi adjectives ending in -आ, past participles agree with the noun:
  MASCULINE SINGULAR: टूटा हुआ शीशा = broken glass
  FEMININE SINGULAR: टूटी हुई खिड़की = broken window
  MASCULINE PLURAL: टूटे हुए खिलौने = broken toys
  FEMININE PLURAL: टूटी हुई चूड़ियाँ = broken bangles

These forms describe the resulting state of a completed action, not the action itself.

Note: हुआ/हुई can be omitted in casual speech: टूटा शीशा = broken glass.
sections.tip: Past participles describe states resulting from completed actions. Think of them as answering 'what happened to this thing?' — टूटा हुआ शीशा = glass that has broken / glass in a broken state.

examples.hi: खुली हुई किताब मेज़ पर है।
examples.en: The open book is on the table.

examples.hi: बना हुआ खाना फ्रिज में रखो।
examples.en: Put the prepared food in the fridge.

examples.hi: गिरे हुए पत्ते ज़मीन पर थे।
examples.en: The fallen leaves were on the ground.

exercises.prompt: "टूटी हुई खिड़की" — participle स्त्रीलिंग क्यों है?
options: Because खिड़की is feminine, and the participle agrees with it
exercises.explanation: The past participle agrees with the noun it modifies. खिड़की (window) is feminine, so टूटा becomes टूटी and हुआ becomes हुई.

exercises.prompt: _____ हुआ खाना फ्रिज में रखो। (Put the prepared food in the fridge.)
exercises.explanation: खाना is masculine singular, so the past participle of बनना takes the masculine form: बना हुआ.

sections.title: Obligative Participle (विध्यर्थक कृदंत) — ना / नी / ने
sections.explanation:
The obligative participle expresses obligation, necessity, or something that 'is to be done'. It is formed from the verb infinitive and functions as an adjective.

FORMATION:
Verb stem + ना/नी/ने (same as infinitive, but used as an adjective)

The form agrees with the noun it modifies:
  MASCULINE SINGULAR: करने का काम = work to be done (oblique because of का)
  FEMININE SINGULAR: पढ़ने की किताब = a book to be read
  MASCULINE PLURAL: देखने के मंदिर = temples to be seen

COMMON PATTERNS:

1. VERB + ना/नी/ने + वाला/वाली/वाले = 'one who does / is about to do':
  जाने वाला = one who is about to go (masculine)
  आने वाली ट्रेन = the arriving train / the train that is about to come
  पढ़ने वाले बच्चे = children who study

2. VERB + ने + को/के लिए = 'in order to / for doing':
  खाने के लिए = for eating / in order to eat
  पढ़ने के लिए = for reading / in order to read

3. VERB + ना/नी/ने as adjective with होना = obligation:
  मुझे काम करना है। = I have to work. (करना agrees with काम, masculine)
  मुझे किताब पढ़नी है। = I have to read a book. (पढ़नी agrees with किताब, feminine)
  मुझे पत्र लिखने हैं। = I have to write letters. (लिखने agrees with पत्र, masculine plural)
sections.tip: When the obligative participle is used with होना (to have to), the verb form agrees with the object, NOT the subject. मुझे किताब पढ़नी है — पढ़नी agrees with किताब (feminine), not with मुझे.

examples.en: I have to read a book. (पढ़नी agrees with किताब, feminine)
I have to write three letters. (लिखने agrees with पत्र, masculine plural)
There are many places to see.

exercises.prompt: "मुझे किताब पढ़नी है" में पढ़नी स्त्रीलिंग क्यों है?
options: Because किताब is feminine and the obligative agrees with the object
exercises.explanation: In obligative constructions with होना, the verbal form agrees with the object. किताब is feminine, so पढ़ना becomes पढ़नी.

exercises.prompt: मुझे काम _____ है। (I have to work.)
exercises.hint: Obligative form of करना, agreeing with काम (masculine singular)
exercises.explanation: काम is masculine singular, so the obligative form of करना is करना (masculine singular): मुझे काम करना है.

exercises.prompt: "आने वाली ट्रेन" — what does वाली indicate?
options: The train that has already arrived
The train that is about to come / the coming train
exercises.explanation: Verb stem + ने + वाला/वाली/वाले means 'one who does' or 'one who is about to do'. आने वाली ट्रेन = the train that is about to come / the coming train. वाली is feminine to agree with ट्रेन.
